overview.short: Mukkoottuthara is a developing town located in the south eastern part of Kottayam district of Kerala State, India.
overview.description: Mukkoottuthara is a developing town located in the south eastern part of Kottayam district of Kerala State, India. Sabarimala is only 48 km (30 mi) from Erumely while travelling through Mukkoottuthara. The roads passing through here are upgraded and maintained as state highways. The main cultivation is rubber.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Kollamula
topPlaces.description: Kollamula is a village in Pathanamthitta district in the state of Kerala, India. It is one of the 11 villages in Ranni taluk.

topPlaces.name: Kalaketty
topPlaces.description: Kalaketty is a village in the Erumely panchayath of Kanjirappally tehsil in the Kottayam district of Kerala state, India. Many Shiva devotees pass through Kalaketty during the Makaravilakku festival period, as the local temple is an important landmark as well as an idathavalam for them.

topPlaces.name: Vechoochira
topPlaces.description: Vechoochira is a village located in Thiruvalla Revenue Division of Pathanamthitta district of Kerala, India. The nearest Railway Station is Thiruvalla, located 45km away from the village.

topPlaces.name: Perunthenaruvi Falls
topPlaces.description: Perunthenaruvi Waterfalls are waterfalls 36 km (22 mi) from Pathanamthitta in Pathanamthitta District, Central Travancore region, Kerala State, India. It is a popular tourist destination situated in Vechoochira Panchayat of Ranni taluk.

topPlaces.name: Perunthenaruvi Weir
topPlaces.description: Perunthenaruvi Weir is a diversion dam built across Pamba river at villages of Naranammoozhy and Vechoochira in Pathanamthitta District of Kerala, India. Perumthenaruvi Small Hydroelectric Project 6 MW (2×3) is a run-of-the-river scheme in river Pamba, and the weir is a part of this scheme.

topPlaces.name: Punchavayal
topPlaces.description: Punchavayal is a village in Mundakayam panchayath of Kanjirappally taluk in the Indian state of Kerala. It is approximately 6 km away by road from Mundakayam, and is at the border of Kottayam and Idukki districts.

topPlaces.name: Panakkachira
topPlaces.description: Panakkachira is a small village in Kanjirappally taluk of Kottayam District, Kerala, India. The village is approximately 7 km (4.3 mi) southeast of Mundakayam.

topPlaces.name: Chathanthara
topPlaces.description: Chathanthara is a small village in Vechoochira panchayath of Ranni taluk, Kerala state, India. It is about 10 km (6.2 mi) from Erumely, 18 km (11 mi) from Ranni and 7 km (4.3 mi) from Vechoochira.

items.note: Kollamula is a village in Pathanamthitta district in the state of Kerala, India. It is one of the 11 villages in Ranni taluk.

Vechoochira is a village located in Thiruvalla Revenue Division of Pathanamthitta district of Kerala, India. The nearest Railway Station is Thiruvalla, located 45km away from the village.

Perunthenaruvi Weir is a diversion dam built across Pamba river at villages of Naranammoozhy and Vechoochira in Pathanamthitta District of Kerala, India. Perumthenaruvi Small Hydroelectric Project 6 MW (2×3) is a run-of-the-river scheme in river Pamba, and the weir is a part of this scheme.

Punchavayal is a village in Mundakayam panchayath of Kanjirappally taluk in the Indian state of Kerala. It is approximately 6 km away by road from Mundakayam, and is at the border of Kottayam and Idukki districts.
Panakkachira is a small village in Kanjirappally taluk of Kottayam District, Kerala, India. The village is approximately 7 km (4.3 mi) southeast of Mundakayam.
Chathanthara is a small village in Vechoochira panchayath of Ranni taluk, Kerala state, India. It is about 10 km (6.2 mi) from Erumely, 18 km (11 mi) from Ranni and 7 km (4.3 mi) from Vechoochira.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Mukkoottuthara Thiruvambadi Sree Krishaswami Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Mukkoottuthara Thiruvambadi Sree Krishaswami Temple is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Mukkoottuthara Thiruvambadi Sree Krishaswami Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Cochin International Airport, Kochi (94 km) and the nearest railway station is Ernakulam Junction (Kochi) (87 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Mukkoottuthara Thiruvambadi Sree Krishaswami Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Mukkoottuthara Thiruvambadi Sree Krishaswami Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Mukkoottuthara Thiruvambadi Sree Krishaswami Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Mukkoottuthara Thiruvambadi Sree Krishaswami Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Mukkoottuthara Thiruvambadi Sree Krishaswami Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kollamula, Kalaketty, Vechoochira, Perunthenaruvi Falls, Perunthenaruvi Weir.
